segments.text: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying,
Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Also put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, and his grain money.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
As soon as the morning dawned, the men were sent away, they and their donkeys.
When they had gone out of the city, and were not yet far off, Joseph said to his steward,
Get up, follow the men; and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Is not this the one from which my lord drinks, and with which he indeed practices divination? You have done evil in so doing.'
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So he overtook them, and he spoke to them these same words.
Why does my lord say these words? Far be it from us that your servants should do such a thing.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: Look, we brought back to you from the land of Canaan the money which we found in the mouth of our sacks. How then could we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: With whomever of your servants it is found, let him die, and we also will be my lord's slaves.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Brothers

segments.text: Now also let it be according to your words; he with whom it is found shall be my slave, and you shall be blameless.
segments.speaker: Joseph's Steward

segments.text: Then each man speedily let down his sack to the ground, and each opened his sack.
So he searched. He began with the oldest and left off with the youngest; and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
Then they tore their clothes, and each man loaded his donkey and returned to the city.
So Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, and he was still there; and they fell before him on the ground.
What deed is this you have done? Did you not know that such a man as I can certainly practice divination?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how shall we clear ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants; here we are, my lord's slaves, both we and he also with whom the cup was found.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Far be it from me that I should do so; the man in whose hand the cup was found, he shall be my slave. And as for you, go up in peace to your father.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: O my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's hearing, and do not let your anger burn against your servant; for you are even like Pharaoh.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: My lord asked his servants, saying, 'Have you a father or a brother?'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, who is young; his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Then you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And we said to my lord, 'The lad cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: But you said to your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall see my face no more.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: So it was, when we went up to your servant my father, that we told him the words of my lord.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And our father said, 'Go back and buy us a little food.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: But we said, 'We cannot go down; if our youngest brother is with us, then we will go down; for we may not see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Then your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons;
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: and the one went out from me, and I said, 'Surely he is torn to pieces'; and I have not seen him since.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: But if you take this one also from me, and calamity befalls him, you shall bring down my gray hair with sorrow to the grave.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Now therefore, when I come to your servant my father, and the lad is not with us, since his life is bound up in the lad's life,
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: it will happen, when he sees that the lad is not with us, that he will die. So your servants will bring down the gray hair of your servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For your servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father forever.'
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the lad as a slave to my lord, and let the lad go up with his brothers.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For how shall I go up to my father if the lad is not with me, lest perhaps I see the evil that would come upon my father?
segments.speaker: Judah
